WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES (TRANSACTION ID # 100109304) FILED BY DEFENDANT LEROUX, ALBERT LOUISNELS AN INDIVIDUAL MGC, INC., DBA MISSION GLASS COMPANY HEARING SET FOR SEP-28-2020 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE SEP-28-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALBERT LEROUX, MGC, INC., DBA MISSION GLASS COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. NO TIMELY OPPOSITION FILED. MOREOVER, THE WITHDRAWAL OF COUNSEL DID NOT OBVIATE THE NEED TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY. OBJECTIONS AND THE RIGHT TO PRODUCE WRITINGS INSTEAD OF RESPONDING TO INTERROGATORIES ARE WAIVED. CCP 2030.290, 2031.300. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTION IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $570 (3 HOURS AT $170/HOUR PLUS $60 FILING FEE). CCP 2030.290(C), 2031.300(C). RESPONSES TO THE INTERROGATORIES AND DOCUMENT REQUESTS MUST BE SERVED BY OCTOBER 29, 2020. SANCTIONS PAYABLE NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 29, 2020. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING THE RULING VERBATIM AND E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT SBORROWMAN@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions Of Dismissal Of The Complaint And For Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff For Disobedience Of Court Orders
Off Calendar Dec-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALBERT LEROUX, MGC, INC., DBA MISSION GLASS COMPANY's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Of Dismissal Of The Complaint And For Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff For Disobedience Of Court Orders:

Transferred to be heard on the same day at 9:00 am in Dept. 301. Dept. 301 handles all discovery matters.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions Of Dismissal Of The Complaint And For Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff For Disobedience Of Court Orders
Off Calendar Dec-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:

Attorney, Frances H. Yoshimura of the Law Offices of John A. Biard (Tel: 925.945.4491; email: fyoshimu@travelers.com) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The judge Pro Tem and counsel authorize the court clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendants' unopposed Motion For Terminating Sanctions Of Dismissal Of The Complaint And For Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff For Disobedience Of Court Orders is GRANTED in its entirety. Terminating sanctions and monetary sanctions of $800 are granted. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven B. Stein: Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported (Disc302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-21-2020 Set For Trial On May-10-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Sep-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALBERT LEROUX, MGC, INC., DBA MISSION GLASS COMPANY's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion to compel is GRANTED. No timely opposition filed. Moreover, the withdrawal of counsel did not obviate the need to respond to discovery. Objections and the right to produce writings instead of responding to interrogatories are waived. CCP 2030.290, 2031.300. The request for sanction is GRANTED in the amount of $570 (3 hours at $170/hour plus $60 filing fee). CCP 2030.290(c), 2031.300(c). Responses to the interrogatories and document requests must be served by October 29, 2020. Sanctions payable no later than October 29, 2020.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating the ruling verbatim and e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
